payload.title: Client work reveals polycentric locus routing across service, data, and ELT Sites
payload.summary: The Staccato setup showed that client work naturally wants multiple authority loci: client service, local data, ELT, User Site, and Narada proper. A single client Site would smear authority; the coherent flow is polycentric locus routing.
observations: Client work naturally wants multiple loci: client-service, data, ELT, User Site, and Narada proper.
Information flow is polycentric and locus-routed, not hierarchical.
All relevant loci must not become broadcast semantics; route to the locus whose authority can interpret or act.
Client-sensitive folders need defensive Git defaults: deny by default, then explicitly admit governance paths.
Local data and ELT Sites may need Narada metadata without becoming Git repos.
Operator/agent help should route to a durable User Site intake path, not to a specific assistant identity.
Simple authored Markdown envelopes remain the right initial substrate when canonical Site-local inbox/task machinery is not yet installed.
The repeating pattern across project, client-service, data, and ELT loci suggests Narada proper wants a documented Site-locus taxonomy and setup templates, but not necessarily new machinery yet.
payload.routing_rule: Route each observation, request, or proposal to the locus whose authority can interpret or act on it. Do not broadcast everywhere.
candidate_future_work: Document a Site-locus taxonomy covering User, PC, Project, Client Service, Data, and ELT loci.
Provide setup templates for client-service repos, local data Sites, and ELT sides.
Keep template output markdown-first and defensive around client-sensitive artifacts.
Avoid implementing heavier Site-local inbox/task machinery until the workflow earns it or Narada proper exposes a stable substrate.
